chunks.text: Overview of the Viptos Wireless Sensor Networks Development Platform Viptos is an interface between TinyOS and Ptolemy II that provides a graphical user interface for TinyOS developers. TinyOS is an event-driven operating system designed for sensor n

vent-driven operating system designed for sensor network nodes that have very limited resources (e.g., 8K bytes of program memory, 512 bytes of RAM). TinyOS, is used, for example, on the Berkeley MICA motes, which are small wireless sensor nodes. ne

A motes, which are small wireless sensor nodes. nesC is an extension to the C programming language designed to embody the structuring concepts and execution model of TinyOS. Included tools nc2moml $PTII/ptolemy/domains/ptinyos/util/nc2moml/nc2moml is

II/ptolemy/domains/ptinyos/util/nc2moml/nc2moml is used to convert nesC files (.nc) into MoML (.moml) files. This will create the Ptolemy II libraries of components that are used to assemble models. TinyOS provides a rich library of nesC components

TinyOS provides a rich library of nesC components. If you install TinyOS 1.x in $PTII/vendors/ptinyos/tinyos-1.x , then the Ptolemy II configure script will find it and automatically make the TinyOS libraries available. ncapp2moml $PTII/ptolemy/doma

libraries available. ncapp2moml $PTII/ptolemy/domains/ptinyos/util/ncapp2moml/ncapp2moml is used to convert nesC application files (.nc) into MoML (.moml) files. This will create Ptolemy II models that you can run in Viptos. You must setup the
s that you can run in Viptos. You must setup the necessary MoML library of nesC components with the nc2moml tool before you can successfully open files made with this ncapp2moml tool.
